text: The transformer feedforward sublayer is a simple yet powerful component of the transformer architecture.
Starting with an input vector of dimension 512, the data first passes through a linear transformation using weight matrix W1 and bias B1.
W1 has dimensions 2048 by 512, expanding the representation from 512 to 2048 dimensions. This expansion allows the network to learn richer features.
Next, the ReLU activation function is applied. ReLU introduces non-linearity by setting all negative values to zero, enabling the model to learn complex patterns.
The activated hidden state then passes through a second linear transformation. Weight matrix W2, with dimensions 512 by 2048, projects the data back down to the original 512 dimensions.
Bias B2 is added to produce the final output. This up-projection followed by down-projection creates a bottleneck architecture that enhances the model's representational capacity.
The complete formula is FFN of x equals W2 times ReLU of W1 times x plus b1, plus b2.
